hero.lede: QS Technology was founded in 2014 in Ho Chi Minh City by a team of electronics and mechanical engineers, with the goal of mastering CNC control hardware for the domestic precision-engineering industry. Today, six QS controller lines run on more than 800 production lines.

story.heading: The QS story
story.p1: In 2014, three engineers at a machine shop in Tan Binh decided to write their own firmware for a CNC milling machine after an imported controller failed and they had to wait half a year for parts. The F-series was born from that real-world need.
story.p2: After two years of development, the first F54 controller was installed at an aluminium-door workshop in Ho Chi Minh City — and has run continuously ever since. From that first product, QS expanded into the Astro-series for automation lines, then servo motors and expansion boards to deliver turnkey solutions.
story.p3: Today, QS Technology runs a 4,200 m² factory in Ho Chi Minh City, an 18-engineer R&D team, and a partner network covering all 35 provinces. The philosophy is unchanged:
story.p3strong: hardware we design ourselves, firmware we write ourselves, technical support delivered directly in Vietnam.

items.d: Every QS controller has its PCB designed, components selected, and firmware written by our in-house engineers. No dependence on foreign supply chains for the product core.
The QS technical team is on-site within 24 hours across 35 provinces. Customers talk directly to the people who write the firmware, with no intermediary dealers.
A 24-month hardware warranty and free firmware support for the product's lifetime. Replacement parts stay available for at least 8 years after a product is discontinued.
